leadText: TM Materials are a category of items introduced in Generation IX. They are primarily used to create TMs, each of which require a specific set of TM Materials to be made.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] TM Machine [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , TM Materials can be used in combination with each other and League Points at the TM Machine in a Pokémon Center to create TMs . They can also be exchanged at the TM Machine for League Points, or used as a material for the Item Printer . Some TM Materials are not included in any TM recipes; with the exception of the Gimmighoul Coin , they serve no practical purpose other than to be sold or used by the Item Printer. These include all materials acquired from version-exclusive Pokémon (other than Shieldon in version 3.0.0), as well as those acquired from Ditto , Rotom , Gimmighoul , and various Pokémon native to Kitakami and Blueberry Academy : Ditto Goo Misdreavus Tears Larvitar Claw Gulpin Mucus Bagon Scales Drifloon Gas Stunky Fur Rotom Sparks Deino Scales Skrelp Kelp Clauncher Claw Oranguru Fur Passimian Fur Stonjourner Stone Eiscue Down Dreepy Powder Gimmighoul Coin Poliwag Slime Sentret Fur Hoothoot Feather Spinarak Thread Aipom Hair Gligar Fang Volbeat Fluid Illumise Fluid Mienfoo Claw Grubbin Thread Cutiefly Powder Cramorant Down Morpeko Snack Doduo Down Chinchou Sparks Skarmory Feather Smeargle Paint Plusle Fur Minun Fur Cranidos Spike Shieldon Claw * Inkay Ink Milcery Cream In Version 3.0.0, Shieldon 's dropped material is used to create TM223 ( Metal Sound ), despite the Pokémon being exclusive to Pokémon Violet . This made it impossible for TM223 to be created in Pokémon Scarlet without using the Union Circle , joining other players' Tera Raid Battles , or transferring a Shieldon or Bastiodon from another game to allow wild Ditto to transform into it. However, this was fixed in Version 3.0.1, with the requirement for Shieldon Claws being removed. Other uses [ edit | edit source ] Some TM Materials have other uses, beyond being used to create TMs or Item Printer jobs. Bronzor Fragment: 10 can be traded for Auspicious Armor in Zapapico . S Sinistea Chip: 10 can be traded for Malicious Armor in Zapapico. V Gimmighoul Coin: If the player has 999 Gimmighoul Coins on hand, Gimmighoul will consume them to evolve into Gholdengo when it levels up.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] All TM Materials, except Gimmighoul Coins, share the same description. Games Description S V ZA Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs . Description for Gimmighoul Coins: Games Description S V ZA Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It seems that Gimmighoul treasure and hoard these.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Effect 1.1.1 TM Machine 1.1.2 Other uses 1.2 Description 1.3 Acquisition 1.4 List In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Effect [ edit | edit source ] TM Machine [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , TM Materials can be used in combination with each other and League Points at the TM Machine in a Pokémon Center to create TMs . They can also be exchanged at the TM Machine for League Points, or used as a material for the Item Printer . Some TM Materials are not included in any TM recipes; with the exception of the Gimmighoul Coin , they serve no practical purpose other than to be sold or used by the Item Printer. These include all materials acquired from version-exclusive Pokémon (other than Shieldon in version 3.0.0), as well as those acquired from Ditto , Rotom , Gimmighoul , and various Pokémon native to Kitakami and Blueberry Academy : Ditto Goo Misdreavus Tears Larvitar Claw Gulpin Mucus Bagon Scales Drifloon Gas Stunky Fur Rotom Sparks Deino Scales Skrelp Kelp Clauncher Claw Oranguru Fur Passimian Fur Stonjourner Stone Eiscue Down Dreepy Powder Gimmighoul Coin Poliwag Slime Sentret Fur Hoothoot Feather Spinarak Thread Aipom Hair Gligar Fang Volbeat Fluid Illumise Fluid Mienfoo Claw Grubbin Thread Cutiefly Powder Cramorant Down Morpeko Snack Doduo Down Chinchou Sparks Skarmory Feather Smeargle Paint Plusle Fur Minun Fur Cranidos Spike Shieldon Claw * Inkay Ink Milcery Cream In Version 3.0.0, Shieldon 's dropped material is used to create TM223 ( Metal Sound ), despite the Pokémon being exclusive to Pokémon Violet . This made it impossible for TM223 to be created in Pokémon Scarlet without using the Union Circle , joining other players' Tera Raid Battles , or transferring a Shieldon or Bastiodon from another game to allow wild Ditto to transform into it. However, this was fixed in Version 3.0.1, with the requirement for Shieldon Claws being removed. Other uses [ edit | edit source ] Some TM Materials have other uses, beyond being used to create TMs or Item Printer jobs. Bronzor Fragment: 10 can be traded for Auspicious Armor in Zapapico . S Sinistea Chip: 10 can be traded for Malicious Armor in Zapapico. V Gimmighoul Coin: If the player has 999 Gimmighoul Coins on hand, Gimmighoul will consume them to evolve into Gholdengo when it levels up. Description [ edit | edit source ] All TM Materials, except Gimmighoul Coins, share the same description. Games Description S V ZA Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It can be used to make TMs . Description for Gimmighoul Coins: Games Description S V ZA Material accidentally dropped by a Pokémon. It seems that Gimmighoul treasure and hoard these. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet , TM Materials are dropped by wild Pokémon upon being defeated or captured, with the type of TM Material being based on the Pokémon's corresponding evolutionary line (or the evolutionary line of the Pokémon it has transformed into, unless the Pokémon was from a Tera Raid Battle ). The amount dropped can be increased for a specific type of Pokémon by eating a food item with the Item Drop Power . Gimmighoul Coins can also be obtained by interacting with Roaming Gimmighoul in the overworld. If the player collects more Gimmighoul Coins after the cap of 999, the extras will be transferred to a Janitor in Medali who will give the player the coins once the player has room. Additionally, some TM Materials can be obtained as gifts in-game. There are no corresponding materials for first partner Pokémon , Legendary Pokémon , Paradox Pokémon , and Pokémon not in any of the game's three Pokédexes, except for regional forms and Hisuian Evolutions , which are technically able to drop TM Materials, despite some of them not appearing in the wild. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] All TM Materials are sold for double the price as League Points , compared to Pokémon Dollars , except the Gimmighoul Coin , which is sold for twenty times the price as Pokémon Dollars. Gimmighoul is the only Pokémon whose Evolution requires TM Materials. Luvdisc 's Material being called Luvdisc Scales may be a reference to the Heart Scale , an item often associated with Luvdisc.
